target.name: Ephos
target.founder: Andrea Rocchetto, Francesco Ceccarelli, Ciro Pentangelo

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Ephos is a seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Andrea Rocchetto, Francesco Ceccarelli, and Ciro Pentangelo, having raised €41.5M in EU funding plus $8.5M in seed capital. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set spanning 3 public semiconductor players (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Etched (Series A, $5.0B), d-Matrix (Series A, $2.0B), Substrate (Series A, $1.0B), and Rain AI (Series A, $500M)—establish a median valuation of $1.5B with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 24.2x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied absent Ephos's disclosed revenue, a standard limitation for private company analysis.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B (low) to $1.7B (high), centered on $1.5B. This estimate reflects peer-set positioning at the median of adjacent-stage financings in semiconductors, a sector currently valued at high multiples on growth and technical differentiation. Key uncertainties include Ephos's actual technical IP defensibility, path to revenue, and capital efficiency relative to the comparable set—data not present in current filings. The range should be treated as indicative pending deeper due diligence on product-market fit and go-to-market trajectory.
